description: Interpreter FetchName with_LookupProperty — Stale PropertyResult After Symbol.unscopables Getter Self-Redefines to Data Property → Cell::as<GetterSetter>() Type Confusion (CVE-2024-8381). CVE-2024-8381. Type confusion vulnerability (csectype-wildptr, sec-high) in the 'with' statement environment lookup path, rooted in with_LookupProperty in js/src/vm/EnvironmentObject.cpp and FetchName in js/src/vm/Interpreter-inl.h. The bug occurs when an object's property has a getter that replaces itself with a data property, and the object is set as its own Symbol.unscopables. During 'with' statement name resolution: (1) LookupProperty finds an own-property getter and populates a PropertyResult indicating it is an accessor, (2) CheckUnscopables calls obj[Symbol.unscopables] which returns the same object, then accesses obj.prop which triggers the getter -- the getter redefines the property as a plain data property and returns false (not unscopable), (3) FetchName receives the now-stale PropertyResult which still claims the property is an accessor. FetchName then calls NativeObject::getGetterSetter (via Cell::as<GetterSetter> in js/src/gc/Cell.h:193) on the slot, which now contains a plain JS value instead of a GetterSetter pointer. In a release build, the engine loads the data value as a pointer, dereferences it to find a supposed getter function, and calls it -- a classic type confusion that enables attacker-controlled function pointer invocation. The fix in changeset 4f1411f1beb6 simplifies with-environment handling in FetchName by forcing a fresh property lookup via the slow path after CheckUnscopables, ensuring the PropertyResult always reflects the current property state.
